prompt: CRISPRi-based toggle: two sgRNAs cross-repressing each other's promoters

expression"}, {"name": "p_lac", "type": "promoter", "description": "Lac-regulated promoter driving dCas9-sgRNA2 expression"}, {"name": "b0034", "type": "rbs", "description": "Standard E. coli RBS for dCas9-sgRNA1"}, {"name": "b0034_2", "type": "rbs", "description": "Standard E. coli RBS for dCas9-sgRNA2"}, {"name": "d_cas9", "type": "cds", "description": "Catalytically dead Cas9 used for CRISPR interference"}, {"name": "sg_rna1", "type": "cds", "description": "sgRNA targeting the p_lac promoter region to repress sgRNA2 transcription"}, {"name": "sg_rna2", "type": "cds", "description": "sgRNA targeting the p_tet promoter region to repress sgRNA1 transcription"}, {"name": "rrn_b", "type": "terminator", "description": "rrnB transcriptional terminator"}, {"name": "op_lac", "type": "operator", "description": "p_lac promoter/operator region targeted by sgRNA2 for CRISPRi repression"}, {"name": "op_tet", "type": "operator", "description": "p_tet promoter/operator region targeted by sgRNA1 for CRISPRi repression"}], "interactions": [{"from": "p_tet", "to": "d_cas9", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "p_tet", "to": "sg_rna1", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "b0034", "to": "d_cas9", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "b0034_2", "to": "sg_rna1", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "p_lac", "to": "d_cas9", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "p_lac", "to": "sg_rna2", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "b0034", "to": "d_cas9", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "b0034_2", "to": "sg_rna2", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "d_cas9", "to": "sg_rna1", "type": "complex_formation"}, {"from": "d_cas9", "to": "sg_rna2", "type": "complex_formation"}, {"from": "sg_rna1", "to": "op_tet", "type": "repression"}, {"from": "sg_rna2", "to": "op_lac", "type": "repression"}], "behavior": "This CRISPRi-based toggle uses two mutually repressing sgRNA branches in E. coli. The p_tet branch expresses dCas9 and sgRNA1, while the p_lac branch expresses dCas9 and sgRNA2. sgRNA1 targets the p_tet promoter/operator region to repress its own branch, and sgRNA2 targets the p_lac promoter/operator region to repress its branch. Because each sgRNA is paired with dCas9, the cross-repression creates a bistable toggle: one promoter dominates while the other is silenced, and the state can be switched by transient induction of the opposing input.", "name": "CRISPRi-based toggle"}

prompt: Mammalian toggle switch between two states using TetR and PipR with CMV-driven readouts
response: {"name": "Mammalian toggle switch between two states using TetR and PipR with CMV-driven readouts", "organism": "Homo sapiens", "components": [{"name": "p_tet_off", "type": "promoter", "description": "Tet-responsive promoter repressed by TetR; drives the opposing regulator in the toggle switch."}, {"name": "p_tet_on", "type": "promoter", "description": "Tet-responsive promoter activated by rtTA; drives the opposing regulator in the toggle switch."}, {"name": "p_cmv_tetr", "type": "promoter", "description": "CMV constitutive promoter driving TetR expression."}, {"name": "p_cmv_pipr", "type": "promoter", "description": "CMV constitutive promoter driving PipR expression."}, {"name": "p_cmv_gfp", "type": "promoter", "description": "CMV constitutive promoter driving GFP reporter expression."}, {"name": "p_cmv_rfp", "type": "promoter", "description": "CMV constitutive promoter driving RFP reporter expression."}, {"name": "rbs_tetr", "type": "rbs", "description": "Kozak translation initiation sequence for TetR."}, {"name": "rbs_pipr", "type": "rbs", "description": "Kozak translation initiation sequence for PipR."}, {"name": "rbs_gfp", "type": "rbs", "description": "Kozak translation initiation sequence for GFP."}, {"name": "rbs_rfp", "type": "rbs", "description": "Kozak translation initiation sequence for RFP."}, {"name": "tetr", "type": "cds", "description": "TetR repressor protein; represses p_tet_off and is the active state regulator in one toggle arm."}, {"name": "pipr", "type": "cds", "description": "PipR repressor protein; represses p_tet_on and is the active state regulator in the opposing toggle arm."}, {"name": "gfp", "type": "cds", "description": "Green fluorescent protein reporter for the TetR-dominant state."}, {"name": "rfp", "type": "cds", "description": "Red fluorescent protein reporter for the PipR-dominant state."}, {"name": "bgh_poly_a_tetr", "type": "terminator", "description": "BGH polyadenylation signal for TetR transcript termination."}, {"name": "bgh_poly_a_pipr", "type": "terminator", "description": "BGH polyadenylation signal for PipR transcript termination."}, {"name": "bgh_poly_a_gfp", "type": "terminator", "description": "BGH polyadenylation signal for GFP transcript termination."}, {"name": "bgh_poly_a_rfp", "type": "terminator", "description": "BGH polyadenylation signal for RFP transcript termination."}, {"name": "p_tet_off_operator", "type": "operator", "description": "Tet operator site associated with p_tet_off and bound by TetR."}, {"name": "p_tet_on_operator", "type": "operator", "description": "Tet operator site associated with p_tet_on and bound by PipR."}], "interactions": [{"from": "p_cmv_tetr", "to": "tetr", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "rbs_tetr", "to": "tetr", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "p_cmv_pipr", "to": "pipr", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "rbs_pipr", "to": "pipr", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "p_cmv_gfp", "to": "gfp", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "rbs_gfp", "to": "gfp", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "p_cmv_rfp", "to": "rfp", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "rbs_rfp", "to": "rfp", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "tetr", "to": "p_tet_off_operator", "type": "repression"}, {"from": "pipr", "to": "p_tet_on_operator", "type": "repression"}], "behavior": "This mammalian toggle switch uses mutual repression between TetR and PipR to create two stable expression states. TetR represses the p_tet_off operator, while PipR represses the p_tet_on operator, establishing a bistable regulatory circuit. CMV-driven GFP and RFP reporters provide readout of the dominant state, with TetR dominance corresponding to one fluorescent output and PipR dominance to the other."}

response: {"name": "Elowitz repressilator", "organism": "Escherichia coli", "components": [{"name": "p_laci", "type": "promoter", "description": "Promoter driving lacI expression; repressed by TetR."}, {"name": "p_tetr", "type": "promoter", "description": "Promoter driving tetR expression; repressed by CI."}, {"name": "p_ci", "type": "promoter", "description": "Promoter driving cI expression; repressed by LacI."}, {"name": "p_gfp", "type": "promoter", "description": "Promoter driving GFP reporter expression; represses the CI promoter."}, {"name": "tetr", "type": "cds", "description": "TetR repressor protein; represses the lacI promoter."}, {"name": "ci", "type": "cds", "description": "Lambda CI repressor protein; represses the tetR promoter and the GFP reporter promoter."}, {"name": "gfp", "type": "cds", "description": "Green fluorescent protein reporter."}, {"name": "rrnB_laci", "type": "terminator", "description": "rrnB transcription terminator for the lacI cassette."}, {"name": "rrnB_tetr", "type": "terminator", "description": "rrnB transcription terminator for the tetR cassette."}, {"name": "rrnB_ci", "type": "terminator", "description": "rrnB transcription terminator for the cI cassette."}, {"name": "rrnB_gfp", "type": "terminator", "description": "rrnB transcription terminator for the gfp cassette."}], "interactions": [{"from": "p_laci", "to": "laci", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "rbs_laci", "to": "laci", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "p_tetr", "to": "tetr", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "rbs_tetr", "to": "tetr", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "p_ci", "to": "ci", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "rbs_ci", "to": "ci", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "p_gfp", "to": "gfp", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "rbs_gfp", "to": "gfp", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "tetr", "to": "p_laci", "type": "repression"}, {"from": "ci", "to": "p_tetr", "type": "repression"}, {"from": "laci", "to": "p_ci", "type": "repression"}, {"from": "ci", "to": "p_gfp", "type": "repression"}], "behavior": "This is the Elowitz repressilator in E. coli, a three-node transcriptional oscillator built from LacI, TetR, and lambda CI arranged in a cyclic repression topology. TetR represses the lacI promoter, LacI represses the cI promoter, and CI represses the tetR promoter, creating delayed negative feedback that generates oscillations in repressor concentrations. A fourth CI-repressible GFP reporter tracks the oscillatory state of the circuit."}

prompt: Negative autoregulation of TetR reducing noise in downstream GFP
response: {"name": "Negative autoregulation of TetR reducing noise in downstream GFP", "organism": "Escherichia coli", "components": [{"name": "p_tet", "type": "promoter", "description": "TetR-repressible promoter driving tetR expression for negative autoregulation."}, {"name": "rbs_tetr", "type": "rbs", "description": "B0034 ribosome binding site for tetR translation."}, {"name": "tetr", "type": "cds", "description": "TetR repressor protein that represses p_tet and also represses p_tet_out to reduce noise in downstream GFP expression."}, {"name": "rrnB", "type": "terminator", "description": "rrnB transcriptional terminator for the tetR cassette."}, {"name": "p_tet_out", "type": "promoter", "description": "TetR-repressible promoter driving downstream GFP expression."}, {"name": "rbs_gfp", "type": "rbs", "description": "B0034 ribosome binding site for gfp translation."}, {"name": "gfp", "type": "cds", "description": "Green fluorescent protein reporter."}, {"name": "rrnB_2", "type": "terminator", "description": "rrnB transcriptional terminator for the gfp cassette."}], "interactions": [{"from": "p_tet", "to": "tetr", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "rbs_tetr", "to": "tetr", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "tetr", "to": "p_tet", "type": "repression"}, {"from": "p_tet_out", "to": "gfp", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "rbs_gfp", "to": "gfp", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "tetr", "to": "p_tet_out", "type": "repression"}], "behavior": "TetR is expressed from p_tet and represses its own promoter, creating negative autoregulation that reduces expression noise. TetR also represses p_tet_out, which drives GFP; the autoregulatory buffering of TetR levels dampens fluctuations in downstream GFP output."}

prompt: Beta-carotene production pathway: CrtE, CrtB, CrtI, CrtY expressed from pBAD arabinose-inducible operon
response: {"name": "Beta-carotene production pathway", "organism": "Escherichia coli", "components": [{"name": "pbad", "type": "promoter", "description": "Arabinose-inducible pBAD promoter driving the carotenoid biosynthesis operon."}, {"name": "rbs_crt_operon", "type": "rbs", "description": "Ribosome binding site for the carotenoid biosynthesis operon."}, {"name": "crt_e", "type": "cds", "description": "CrtE, geranylgeranyl diphosphate synthase."}, {"name": "crt_b", "type": "cds", "description": "CrtB, phytoene synthase."}, {"name": "crt_i", "type": "cds", "description": "CrtI, phytoene desaturase."}, {"name": "crt_y", "type": "cds", "description": "CrtY, lycopene cyclase."}, {"name": "rrn_b", "type": "terminator", "description": "rrnB transcription terminator for the carotenoid biosynthesis operon."}], "interactions": [{"from": "pbad", "to": "crt_e", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "pbad", "to": "crt_b", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "pbad", "to": "crt_i", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "pbad", "to": "crt_y", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "rbs_crt_operon", "to": "crt_e", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "rbs_crt_operon", "to": "crt_b", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "rbs_crt_operon", "to": "crt_i", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "rbs_crt_operon", "to": "crt_y", "type": "translation"}], "behavior": "In Escherichia coli, arabinose induction of the pBAD promoter drives expression of a single operon encoding CrtE, CrtB, CrtI, and CrtY. These enzymes act sequentially to convert endogenous precursors into beta-carotene, with CrtE producing geranylgeranyl diphosphate, CrtB forming phytoene, CrtI desaturating phytoene to lycopene, and CrtY cyclizing lycopene to beta-carotene."}

prompt: Dual-input kill switch in E. coli: without both IPTG and arabinose, CcdB toxin kills cells; both inducers maintain CcdA antitoxin
response: {"name": "Dual-input kill switch in E. coli", "organism": "Escherichia coli", "components": [{"name": "p_tet", "type": "promoter", "description": "Tet-regulated promoter driving ccdA antitoxin expression; repressed by TetR and derepressed by IPTG."}, {"name": "p_bad", "type": "promoter", "description": "Arabinose-inducible pBAD promoter driving ccdB toxin expression; activated by AraC in the presence of arabinose."}, {"name": "rbs_ccda", "type": "rbs", "description": "B0034 ribosome binding site for ccdA translation."}, {"name": "rbs_ccdb", "type": "rbs", "description": "B0034 ribosome binding site for ccdB translation."}, {"name": "ccda", "type": "cds", "description": "CcdA antitoxin that neutralizes CcdB toxin and permits cell survival."}, {"name": "ccdb", "type": "cds", "description": "CcdB toxin that poisons DNA gyrase and kills E. coli cells."}, {"name": "t_rrnb_ccda", "type": "terminator", "description": "rrnB transcription terminator for the ccdA cassette."}, {"name": "t_rrnb_ccdb", "type": "terminator", "description": "rrnB transcription terminator for the ccdB cassette."}], "interactions": [{"from": "p_tet", "to": "ccda", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "rbs_ccda", "to": "ccda", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "p_bad", "to": "ccdb", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "rbs_ccdb", "to": "ccdb", "type": "translation"}], "behavior": "This E. coli kill switch uses two inducible expression modules to implement a dual-input survival logic. The ccdB toxin is expressed from pBAD and is intended to kill cells unless arabinose is present, while ccdA antitoxin is expressed from pTet and is intended to protect cells unless IPTG is present. In the prompt's logic, survival requires both inducers so that CcdA is produced to neutralize CcdB; without both inputs, the intended behavior is that toxin activity dominates and cells die. The design assumes IPTG derepresses pTet to produce CcdA and arabinose activates pBAD to produce CcdB, with the net outcome depending on the relative expression balance set by the two inducers."}

prompt: Engineer a tumor-targeting E. coli Nissle circuit: hypoxia-responsive FNR promoter drives InvA; constitutive anti-HER2 nanobody surface-displayed via INP anchor

The design uses two independent transcription units in E. coli; each CDS is supported by a promoter, RBS, and terminator, and the regulatory logic matches the specified tumor-targeting and hypoxia-responsive behavior."}

prompt: Yeast galactose utilization rewiring: GAL80 knockout with GAL4-VP64 driving constitutive GAL1/7/10 expression

prompt: CRISPR-Cas13-based RNA sensor: Cas13 cleaves a quenched GFP mRNA when trigger RNA is present
response: {"name": "CRISPR-Cas13-based RNA sensor", "organism": "Escherichia coli", "components": [{"name": "p_j23100", "type": "promoter", "description": "Constitutive J23100 promoter driving Cas13 expression"}, {"name": "rbs_cas13", "type": "rbs", "description": "B0034 ribosome binding site for Cas13 translation"}, {"name": "cas13", "type": "cds", "description": "Cas13 RNA-targeting nuclease that cleaves target RNA when guided by a cognate crRNA"}, {"name": "t_rrnb", "type": "terminator", "description": "rrnB transcriptional terminator"}, {"name": "p_j23100_2", "type": "promoter", "description": "Constitutive J23100 promoter driving the quenched GFP reporter transcript"}, {"name": "rbs_gfp", "type": "rbs", "description": "B0034 ribosome binding site for GFP translation"}, {"name": "quenched_gfp", "type": "cds", "description": "GFP reporter transcript containing an embedded Cas13 target site; cleavage of the RNA by Cas13 reduces GFP expression"}, {"name": "t_rrnb_2", "type": "terminator", "description": "rrnB transcriptional terminator"}, {"name": "p_j23100_3", "type": "promoter", "description": "Constitutive J23100 promoter driving crRNA expression"}, {"name": "rbs_crrna", "type": "rbs", "description": "B0034 ribosome binding site for crRNA transcription"}, {"name": "crRNA", "type": "cds", "description": "Guide RNA that directs Cas13 to the target sequence in the quenched GFP transcript"}, {"name": "t_rrnb_3", "type": "terminator", "description": "rrnB transcriptional terminator"}], "interactions": [{"from": "p_j23100", "to": "cas13", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "rbs_cas13", "to": "cas13", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "p_j23100_2", "to": "quenched_gfp", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "rbs_gfp", "to": "quenched_gfp", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "p_j23100_3", "to": "crRNA", "type": "transcription"}, {"from": "rbs_crrna", "to": "crRNA", "type": "translation"}, {"from": "cas13", "to": "crRNA", "type": "complex_formation"}, {"from": "cas13", "to": "quenched_gfp", "type": "inhibition"}], "behavior": "Constitutive expression of Cas13 and a guide crRNA creates a CRISPR-Cas13 RNA-targeting system in E. coli. When the guide RNA is present, Cas13 forms a ribonucleoprotein complex that recognizes and cleaves the embedded target site within the quenched GFP transcript, reducing reporter output. This RNA-sensing behavior is implemented as a constitutive Cas13-crRNA module that inhibits the GFP reporter transcript."}
